meta_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Claude Code Skills to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your MVP. Use Claude Code and Antigravity to turn abstract concepts into revenue-generating SaaS products. A beginner's guide for founders.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' trap is just a lack of execution framework. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you transform abstract pain points into structured, production-ready codebases. Focus on solving one specific vertical problem for a niche audience, and let AI handle the heavy lifting of your initial MVP architecture.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the graveyard of ambition. You have the drive to build, but you are paralyzed by the 'what'—the elusive perfect idea that hasn't been built yet. This isn't a lack of creativity; it is a lack of a feedback loop between your intuition and technical execution.

We are shifting the paradigm. Instead of waiting for a lightning bolt, we use Claude Code to rapidly iterate through market-validated problems. By combining the automation of Antigravity with the reasoning of Claude, you stop dreaming and start shipping. It is time to treat your code as a business asset, not a creative burden.

steps.title: Define Your Niche Problem
steps.description: Identify a high-value, boring business problem to solve. We use Antigravity to generate a market-validated feature list.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS founder. List 5 high-pain, low-complexity problems in the logistics industry that can be solved with a browser-based dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review these 5 ideas and select the one with the highest potential for recurring revenue.'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Schema
steps.description: Before writing a line of code, establish your data structure to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a SaaS MVP tracking logistics shipments. Include tables for users, shipments, and status_logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a prisma schema file based on this structure and initialize a new project.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core UI
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the frontend components based on your schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive React/Tailwind component for a shipment tracking dashboard header and status table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create the dashboard component files in the components/ directory.'

steps.title: Implement Business Logic
steps.description: Connect the frontend to your backend services using secure API endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure API route for fetching shipment data by user ID, including error handling for unauthorized requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the API endpoint in the next.js app router and test the response.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Move from local environment to a live URL for real-world user testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for deploying a Next.js application to Vercel, including environment variable management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify build configuration and provide deployment commands.'

pro_tips: Always start with the database schema; your data model dictates your business logic, not the other way around.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API endpoints before you finish the UI; it forces you to clarify your requirements.
Don't build custom authentication from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase Auth and let Claude integrate the SDKs.
Monitor your token usage by breaking complex features into smaller, modular prompts rather than one monolithic instruction.
Treat your CLI commands as documentation; keep a history file to replicate successful patterns in future projects.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, share it with 10 potential users in your target niche. Collect feedback, iterate using Claude Code, and focus strictly on solving their number one pain point.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. By mastering these tools, you reduce your time-to-revenue by 80%. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
